title: Cathay Pacific Doubles SAF Commitments in Record-Breaking 2025
content: Cathay Pacificâ€™s Corporate Sustainable Aviation Fuel (SAF) Programme achieved a record year in 2025, with commitments more than doubling compared to 2024. Supported by 17 global partners, the initiative secured approximately 17,400 tons of SAF, resulting in a reduction of nearly 54,600 tons of CO2 emissions. Microsoft joined as a new partner, while Kuehne + Nagel remained the largest cargo contributor. A collaboration with DHL Express also enabled the first SAF uplift for Air Hong Kong. Multi-year commitments from partners like EQT, along with support from DSV and EY, underscore a long-term dedication to decarbonization. The expanded usage was facilitated by suppliers including Neste, Shell Aviation, and Sinopec. Beyond corporate partnerships, Cathay launched a digital platform for individual contributions and announced investments to accelerate cleaner fuel production capacity in Asia.
author: Analysts Account
